meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding Projects (Intermediate Guide)
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by building advanced user onboarding flows. Master Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your developer portfolio today.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' paralysis ends when you stop building generic CRUD apps and start solving high-value UX problems. User onboarding is the lifeblood of SaaS; by engineering sophisticated, data-driven signup flows, you demonstrate the exact architectural mastery employers crave, turning a simple project into a powerful career-defining portfolio piece.
content.intro:
The screen stares back at you, a blinking cursor in a void of endless possibilities. You have the skills, the syntax, and the drive, yet the blank canvas of a 'new project' feels like a fortress wall. This is the common purgatory of the intermediate developer: you know how to code, but you don't know what matters. 

Stop building To-Do lists. The real-world value—and the secret to landing your first dev role—lies in solving the friction points that keep businesses awake at night. User onboarding is the ultimate gateway project; it forces you to master state management, asynchronous data flow, and psychological design, transforming your portfolio from 'student hobbyist' to 'serious product engineer.'

steps.title: Define the User Journey Map
steps.description: Before writing code, map the critical path from landing page to 'Aha!' moment. Focus on reducing time-to-value by identifying exactly which three actions a user must take to realize the product's core utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Create a 3-step user onboarding flow for a SaaS project management tool. Define the state machine logic for a progress tracker that persists across page refreshes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a state machine schema for user onboarding progress using React and TypeScript'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Progress Indicators
steps.description: Build a visual onboarding component that persists state. This demonstrates your ability to handle complex front-end architecture and UI/UX synchronization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable React component for an onboarding progress bar that integrates with a Redux or Zustand store. Ensure it handles animation states for step transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a progress-bar component that animates on state change using Framer Motion'

steps.title: Build Context-Aware Tooltips
steps.description: Use overlays to guide users through the interface. This requires precise DOM positioning and event handling, teaching you about portals and z-index management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook 'useOnboardingTooltip' that manages the visibility and positioning of an overlay based on the user's current interaction step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement a tooltip system using React Portals to ensure overlays are not clipped by parent containers'

steps.title: Integrate Data-Driven Personalization
steps.description: Capture user preferences during onboarding and use them to customize the dashboard. This simulates a real production environment where backend state dictates frontend UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a backend service structure for a Node.js API that accepts onboarding survey data and returns a personalized user settings object.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a POST endpoint to handle user onboarding survey responses and update the database schema'

steps.title: Stress-Test with Edge Cases
steps.description: Ensure your onboarding flow survives page reloads, network drops, and aborted sessions. Robustness is what separates a student project from a production-ready application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the onboarding state machine, covering edge cases like session timeouts and interrupted user flows.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'write Jest tests for the onboarding state machine logic to ensure persistence works on page reload'

pro_tips: Never hardcode onboarding steps; fetch them from a configuration JSON so you can A/B test the flow without redeploying.
Use local storage as a fallback for onboarding state to preserve progress even if the user isn't authenticated yet.
Always include a 'Skip' button. Forcing users through a 10-step tutorial is the fastest way to drive churn.
Analytics tracking on onboarding steps is non-negotiable; track the drop-off rate at every single step to find friction points.
Treat your onboarding flow as a separate micro-module within your repo to keep your main dashboard code clean and maintainable.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy the project to Vercel and add a 'Case Study' README section explaining the technical challenges you solved with the progress tracking logic.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a project that actually matters. Stop watching tutorials and start shipping code that solves real problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
